description: You are an electrician commissioning a new three-phase pump motor at a water treatment facility. The motor nameplate reads: 37 kW rated output, 415 V line-to-line, 92% efficiency, 0.86 power factor. The feeder uses a 100/5 A current transformer (CT) and runs through 50 meters of copper cable with 3% maximum allowable voltage drop. Using the Electrical Engineering Calculations app, compute the three-phase power triangle (apparent, real, and reactive power), determine the CT secondary current for relay calibration, and find the minimum cable size for the feeder. End on the cable size calculator screen showing the recommended cable size.
